ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-03-2019 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-22-2019 Continued To Dec-03-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brianna Mannion for San Francisco Public Administrator.
Allison Bellumori appeared by telephone for Objector.

The Court stated that it read the declaration, that it believes the original calculations were correct and that the Public Administrator needs to file an amendment. The Second and Final Account and Report on Status of Administration, etc. is continued to December 3, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Mannion to file an amendment.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-08-2019 Continued To Oct-22-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Brianna Mannion for San Francisco Public Administrator.
Allison Bellumori via Court Call for Brandenburger & Davis.

Ms. Mannion stated she had hoped the court can rule on the petition with consideration of the filed objection, that this case has been dragging, that Ms. Bellumori's office waited until the last second to notify them, and she could submit an amended order with changed percentages. The Court noted there is a fundamental change to the petition, that there are conflicts with the agreement as to whether to calculate the fee on balance after tax, the objector is the bank not the heirs, so the heirs need to be given notice of changed distribution amounts. Ms. Bellumori stated she would like the opportunity to submit a further brief to the court to further evaluate calculations. Ms. Mannion stated this is the first time she has seen value added tax, that the agreement is between the assignees and the heirs, they are free to contract how they want, and request that Ms. Bellumori file her supplemental briefing within the next couple weeks before she files the amended petition. The Court suggested Ms. Bellumori re-calculate the court's way. Ms. Bellumori stated she will propose both calculations.

The Second and Final Report and Account, Petition for Final Distribution is continued to October 22, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-21-2019 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT
Off Calendar Mar-19-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brianna Mannion appearing for Janet Boessenecker on behalf of the San Francisco Public Guardian.

Judge Busch disclosed that until recently Ms. Mannion was employed by the Court in the capacity as a Staff Attorney, and that he does not believe there is any requirement to recuse himself or that it impacts his ability to resolve the matter. Ms. Mannion stated that Ms. Boessenecker asked Scott Hancock (Attorney for 12 Heirs) to appear, that he was expected to appear telephonically and that he did not check in. The Court noted that it does not have a 13100 declaration as to Erika. Ms. Mannion stated that they are not here for final distribution and that notice has been given to everyone. The Court granted the First Account and Report of Status of Administration, etc. (order signed in open Court). The Court informed Ms. Mannion that the issue of Erika's entitlement to Laszlo's interest is to be addressed prior to final distribution. Status on close of estate is set for May 21, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Those Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-12-2018 Continued To Jun-26-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Giulio Accornero for Judith Dry

Mr. Accornero stated that he did not know notice was not given to the post deceased heirs, he thought a petition for entitlement could be verified by the attorney, the petitioner is in Virginia, and the petitioner was notified. The Court inquired if there was a reason the petitioner cannot send in verification. Mr. Accornero stated that the petitioner signed the first petition, he can produce verification if that is what the Court wants, and that he does not have the address for the widow of Laszlo. The Petition to Determine Those Persons Entitled to Distribution of Estate is continued to June 26, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for submission of verification.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: R. Joseph. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Those Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
Off Calendar May-08-2018 Continued To May-29-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Those Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-27-2018 Continued To May-08-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Giulio Accornero for Judith Dry (Petitioner).

The Court informed Mr. Accornero that the matter needs to be continued for him to deal with the outstanding issues that were pointed out by the Probate Examiner. Mr. Accornero stated that he did deal with them, that the only issue is with notice to one of Mr. Hancock's Client and that all parties are represented by Counsel. The Court informed Mr. Accornero that what he filed was a response that does not cure the issues. The Petition to Determine Those Persons Entitled to Distribution of Estate is continued to May 8, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Those Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
Off Calendar Dec-23-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Guilio Accornero for (petitioner) Judith Dry.

The Court stated that there are notice issues, that the other attorneys are not present today and asked if Mr. Accornero had a plan regarding the notice. Mr. Accornero stated that the petition should be taken off calendar and that when the statement of interest is put together he will be able to notice everyone. The Court stated that it does not know of a way to track everyone down, that he can file a new petition when ready and confirmed that the filing of a petition requires notice. The petition to determine those persons entitled to distribution of estate was taken off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not Reported)
